rules: 'dispense' removes units of a drug from stock, consuming the soonest-to-expire batch first (FEFO); an under-filled request records a stockout.; 'reorder' adds a fresh batch of a drug, expiring 'shelf_life_days' after the current day.; 'expire' advances the clock by one day (or 'days') and discards every batch whose expiry day has been reached, adding its units to expired_total.; Stock is never negative and expired units can never be dispensed.
description: a pharmacy inventory world with stock and expiry; actions dispense, reorder, expire

rules: An incident has a priority (1 = most urgent) and a status: waiting -> assigned -> onscene -> resolved.; A unit has a status forming the lifecycle: idle -> enroute -> onscene -> idle.; 'dispatch' sends an idle unit to a waiting incident: the unit becomes enroute and the incident assigned. If params are omitted, the first idle unit is sent to the most urgent waiting incident.; 'arrive' marks an enroute unit onscene; its incident also becomes onscene.; 'return' clears an onscene unit back to idle, resolves its incident, and increments 'resolved'.; Every successful action advances 'time' by one tick; illegal actions are no-ops.; A unit serves at most one incident at a time, and an incident is he
description: an ambulance dispatch world: incidents by priority and units; actions dispatch, arrive, return

rules: Each ward has a fixed bed capacity; occupancy of a ward never exceeds its capacity.; admit: seat a waiting (or perceived 'pending') patient into a ward that has a free bed; with no params, take the head of the waitlist and the lowest-named ward with space. If no bed is available the patient keeps waiting.; transfer: relocate an admitted patient to a different ward that has a free bed; the source frees a bed and the target fills one, so total occupancy is unchanged. With no params, move the lowest-acuity patient from the fullest ward to the ward with the most free space.; discharge: release an admitted patient, freeing a bed in their ward; with no params, discharge the lowest-acuity patient.;
description: an ICU bed-allocation world across wards with bed counts; actions admit, transfer, discharge as beds free up

rules: A visit's lifecycle is waiting -> routed -> completed; it advances one stage per action.; enqueue adds a new visit to the waiting pool with an auto-assigned, monotonically increasing id.; Priority is an integer 1..5 (5 = most urgent), clamped into range; it defaults to 3.; route removes a visit from the waiting pool and appends it to the queue for its own specialty.; route and complete select the most urgent visit first, breaking ties by earliest arrival (lowest id).; complete removes the head of a specialty's provider queue and records it as completed.; num_waiting, num_routed, and num_completed always equal the sizes of their respective structures.; Actions with nothing to act on (e.g. rou
description: a telehealth visit queue routed by specialty; actions enqueue, route, complete

rules: Each step advances 30 minutes of simulated time.; 'dose_insulin' adds its 'units' param (default 1.0) to insulin-on-board (iob).; 'eat' adds its 'carbs' param (default 45.0 g) to carbs-on-board (cob).; Each step half of carbs-on-board digests, raising glucose by 3 mg/dL per gram digested.; Each step 30% of insulin-on-board absorbs, lowering glucose by 40 mg/dL per unit absorbed.; 'exercise' burns 30 mg/dL per unit of intensity (default 1.0) and boosts insulin sensitivity by 50% per unit of intensity for that step.; Endogenous liver output drifts glucose 10% of the way back to a 100 mg/dL baseline each step.; Glucose is clamped to [20, 600] mg/dL; status is hypoglycemia (<70), in_range (70-18
description: a diabetes glucose-management world: blood glucose responds to dose, meals, exercise; actions dose_insulin, eat, exercise
